path_normalization_decision.decision: Later WaveBench commands must target the singular code path wavebench_dataset/time_varying/is/ even though the upstream README still documents wavebench_datasets/.

environment_recommendation.name: wavebench
provisioning_recipe: conda create -y -n wavebench python=3.9 cupy pkg-config libjpeg-turbo opencv pytorch torchvision cudatoolkit=11.3 -c pytorch -c conda-forge
pip install ffcv matplotlib jupyterlab pytorch-lightning einops ml-collections wandb
notes: The upstream README gives an installation recipe but does not include jax or jwave in the pip line even though the forward-model generation path requires them.
No existing local conda environment imported all four required surfaces during this pass.
Later physics validation should extend the upstream recipe with explicit jax and jwave installation before rerunning the import probe.

environment_status.blocking_reason: No local environment imported ffcv, jax, jwave, and ml_collections together during this pass.

fno.reason: Exact public Google Drive checkpoint identifiers were recovered, but the representative official depth-4 checkpoint does not load on the current upstream WaveBench checkout because the saved Fourier weights are 4-D while the current FNO implementation expects 5-D complex-weight tensors.

unet.reason: Exact public Google Drive checkpoint identifiers were recovered, and the representative official ch-32 checkpoint loads successfully with LitModel.load_from_checkpoint(...) on the current upstream WaveBench checkout.

2026-04-29-wavebench-native-baseline-rep.reason: The selected .beton is staged under the stable target, the public Google Drive checkpoint identifiers are now explicit, and the baseline split is durable: native U-Net is checkpoint-reusable while native FNO should run via the recorded retraining route until the checkpoint/code mismatch is resolved.
